title: Today's Tech Brief: Dozens of Red Hat packages backdoored through its official NPM channel
summary: A look at the latest tech news, including a security breach at Red Hat and Casey Neistat's take on social media posting.

body.text: I've got a couple of interesting tech stories to share with you today. First up, it seems that dozens of Red Hat packages were backdoored through its official NPM channel. For those who might not be familiar, NPM is a popular package manager for JavaScript, and Red Hat is a well-known player in the world of enterprise software. This security breach is a big deal because Red Hat's software is widely used in enterprise environments, and a breach like this can have serious consequences.

According to a report from Ars Technica, official Red Hat NPM accounts were compromised and used to push malicious packages. It's a reminder that even the biggest players in the tech industry can fall victim to cyber attacks. I've been following this story and it's interesting to see how Red Hat is responding to the breach.

Also making headlines is Casey Neistat, the legendary YouTuber, who recently shared his guide to posting every day on social media. In an interview with The Vergecast, Neistat talked about how the platform has changed over the years and how to create a lot of content without burning out. I've always been impressed by Neistat's productivity, and it's great to see him sharing his expertise with others.

Dozens of Red Hat packages backdoored through its official NPM channel is also worth a quick mention. The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

Casey Neistat’s guide to posting every day, from theverge.com, is also worth a quick mention. The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
